## The Code: A Micro-BPE in Python

### The Vocabulary Abstraction

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="make_vocab"}
```

`make_vocab` starts with an empty token-to-ID mapping.

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="vocab_extend"}
```

`vocab_extend` returns a vocabulary with one more token ID.

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="vocab_lookup"}
```

`vocab_lookup` retrieves the ID for a token, or `None` if the token is unknown.
Together, these helpers create, extend, and query the mapping from string tokens to integer IDs.

### Characters and Corpus

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="word_to_tokens"}
```

`word_to_tokens` turns one word into characters and appends `"_"` as the end-of-word marker.

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="words_to_corpus"}
```

`words_to_corpus` applies that conversion to every word in the training corpus.

### Counting Pairs

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="imports"}
```

This section uses `Counter` to tally pair frequencies and `Sequence` for read-only sequence type hints.

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="adjacent_pairs"}
```

`adjacent_pairs` lists every neighboring token pair in one token sequence.

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="count_pairs"}
```

`count_pairs` counts those neighboring pairs across the whole corpus.

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="most_frequent"}
```

`most_frequent` chooses the pair with the highest count.

### Applying a Merge

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="merge_pair"}
```

`merge_pair` scans one token sequence and replaces every matching adjacent pair with the joined token.

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="merge_corpus"}
```

`merge_corpus` applies the same merge to every token sequence in the corpus.

### The Training Loop

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="unique_tokens"}
```

`unique_tokens` collects the current vocabulary items from the tokenized corpus.

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="train_bpe"}
```

`train_bpe` repeatedly counts pairs, merges the most frequent pair, records the merge rule, and adds each new token to the vocabulary.

### Encoding

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="encode"}
```

`encode` applies the trained merge rules to a new word and returns the final token IDs.

### Demo

```{.python include="src/python/micro_bpe.py" snippet="demo"}
```

`demo` trains a tiny tokenizer and encodes two example words.
Run this file directly with `python3 src/python/micro_bpe.py`.

Output:

```text
Vocabulary:
  0 -> _
  1 -> d
  2 -> e
  3 -> i
  4 -> l
  5 -> n
  6 -> o
  7 -> r
  8 -> s
  9 -> t
  10 -> w
  11 -> lo
  12 -> low
  13 -> es
  14 -> est
  15 -> est_
  16 -> low_
  17 -> lowe
  18 -> lower
  19 -> lower_

Encoding "low":   [16]
Encoding "lower": [19]
```
